body.title: [dependency] Make the vault device the single credential home; migrate scattered secrets into it
body.intention: I intend that the existing vault device (unseen_university/devices/vault/) becomes THE credential home — provider/application secrets stored encrypted and device-scoped in the vault, fetched via its connect-time client — with uu_bash_profile.sh reduced to only the pre-Postgres DB boot secret, and no plaintext provider keys anywhere in .bashrc/.env.
body.description:
Akien 2026-07-03: 'there already is supposed to be an agent component vault thing in the devices — it is supposed to become that.' Corrected target: the SINGLE credential home is the **vault device** (unseen_university/devices/vault/), NOT uu_bash_profile.sh. The vault is built: Fernet-encrypted at rest (master.key at ~/.unseen_university/vault/master.key, chmod 600), Postgres-backed store, DEVICE-SCOPED access via client.get_credential(device_id, owner, key) with env/flat fallback when the vault is down (unseen_university/devices/vault/{client,store,device}.py). This is connect-time composition per [[reference_compose_credentials_at_connect_time]]. 'It is supposed to become that' => complete its adoption as the cred source.

**Layering (resolves the bootstrap chicken-and-egg):** the vault store is Postgres-backed, so the DB CONNECTION secret cannot live in the vault (the vault needs it to start). uu_bash_profile.sh (chmod 600) keeps ONLY the pre-Postgres boot secret — the DB creds (UU_DB_USER/PASSWORD/IP) needed to reach Postgres/vault. Everything else (provider/application keys) moves INTO the vault.

**Secrets to migrate into the vault:** (1) the OpenRouter key at ~/.bashrc:144 — this is already ticketed as T-uu-bashrc-openrouter-key (triage, 2026-06-22); DO NOT duplicate its rotation, coordinate with it (that ticket is the OR-key instance of this migration). (2) GOOGLE_AI_STUDIO_API_KEY in ~/.unseen_university/.env. Wire the consuming devices/launchers to fetch these via the vault client at connect-time.

**⚠ AKIEN ACTION (blocks):** rotate the exposed OpenRouter key (shared with T-uu-bashrc-openrouter-key) — it has been plaintext in .bashrc and may be in shell history/backups. CC cannot rotate provider keys.

**Discovery in sprint:** confirm the vault is seeded/wired as the live source (device.py/store.py/master.key exist — adoption may be partial; T-vault-settings-ui is related); verify no code still reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY / GOOGLE_AI_STUDIO_API_KEY from env/.bashrc/.env after migration.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/vault/ (seed/store the migrated creds), the consuming devices (fetch via vault client), ~/.bashrc:144 + ~/.unseen_university/.env (remove plaintext — personal, back up first), ~/.unseen_university/uu_bash_profile.sh (reduce to DB boot secret).
**Design rules:** compose-credentials-at-connect-time; no-live-keys-in-source; inference-proxy-only; no-sqlite (vault is Postgres-backed — correct); fail-soft (vault client returns '' + falls back).
**Scope boundary:** IN — migrate the OR + Google keys into the vault, wire consumers to the vault client, reduce uu_bash_profile.sh to the DB boot secret, remove the plaintext provider keys. OUT — the OR-key rotation mechanics (T-uu-bashrc-openrouter-key owns that); the non-secret .bashrc export migration (T-bashrc-uu-migration-finish); deleting the emptied debris files (T-remove-env-creds-debris); a vault settings UI (T-vault-settings-ui).
**Test plan:** integration against the real vault (Postgres) — a device fetches the migrated OR/Google key via client.get_credential and gets the value; an unauthorized device_id gets '' (device-scope enforced). Acceptance: `grep -rE "sk-or-|GOOGLE_AI_STUDIO_API_KEY=" ~/.bashrc ~/.unseen_university/.env` returns nothing; the keys resolve from the vault at connect-time.
**Completion criteria:** provider/app secrets live encrypted+device-scoped in the vault and are fetched via its client; uu_bash_profile.sh holds only the DB boot secret; no plaintext provider keys in .bashrc/.env; the exposed OR key is rotated (Akien).
**Rollback:** vault rows can be deleted and the .bashrc/.env backups restore prior state (manual — outside git).
